description: To receive what is offered or acknowledge what is present, saying yes to reality rather than fighting it. 'Kelomi' names the stance of openness—welcoming gifts, acknowledging truths, and making peace with circumstances as they are.
sound_symbolism: The word opens to receive. 'Ke' begins with velar contact, attention focusing on what is present. Then 'lo' flows with liquid openness, arms extending to receive. Finally 'mi' settles inward with nasal acceptance, what is offered now held.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mi lo themo kelomi' (I accept something). Can express accepting persons: 'mi ru kelomi' (I accept you as you are). Related to whelani (welcome) but emphasizes internal acceptance rather than external hospitality.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Acceptance is the foundation of equanimity—meeting what arises without aversion, working with reality rather than against it.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Accepting others as they are creates space for authentic relationship—we meet the person present, not the person we wish they were.
